title: Teamwork & Collaboration - 50 Phrases
definition: 50 phrases for working well with others - coordinating tasks, offering help, and building a collaborative team culture.

phrases.phrase: Let's divide and conquer
phrases.meaning: Suggests splitting up tasks to work faster.
phrases.example: Let's divide and conquer - you take design, I'll take content.

phrases.phrase: Who's picking this up?
phrases.meaning: Asks who will take on a task.
phrases.example: Who's picking this up before Friday's deadline?

phrases.phrase: I can take this off your plate
phrases.meaning: Offers to handle a task for someone.
phrases.example: I can take this off your plate if you're swamped.

phrases.phrase: Let's sync up before we start
phrases.example: Let's sync up before we start on the new feature.

phrases.phrase: I'll own this piece if you take that one
phrases.example: I'll own this piece if you take that one.

phrases.phrase: Let's make sure we're not duplicating effort
phrases.example: Let's make sure we're not duplicating effort on this.

phrases.phrase: I'll loop you in once I have a draft
phrases.meaning: Promises to share progress at a later stage.
phrases.example: I'll loop you in once I have a draft ready.

phrases.phrase: Can you cover for me while I'm out?
phrases.meaning: Asks someone to handle responsibilities during an absence.
phrases.example: Can you cover for me while I'm out next week?

phrases.phrase: Let's split this into smaller tasks
phrases.example: Let's split this into smaller tasks so it's less overwhelming.

phrases.phrase: I'll take the lead on this part
phrases.meaning: Volunteers to be responsible for a portion of work.
phrases.example: I'll take the lead on this part of the project.

phrases.phrase: Let's coordinate so we don't overlap
phrases.example: Let's coordinate so we don't overlap on outreach.

phrases.phrase: I'll hand this off to you once I'm done
phrases.meaning: Passes work along in a sequence.
phrases.example: I'll hand this off to you once I'm done with my part.

phrases.phrase: Can we pair up on this?
phrases.meaning: Suggests working together directly on a task.
phrases.example: Can we pair up on this to move faster?

phrases.phrase: Let me know if you need a hand
phrases.example: Let me know if you need a hand with the report.

phrases.phrase: Happy to jump in and help
phrases.example: Happy to jump in and help if you're stuck.

phrases.phrase: I've got some bandwidth if you need support
phrases.example: I've got some bandwidth if you need support this week.

phrases.phrase: Don't hesitate to ask for help
phrases.example: Don't hesitate to ask for help if you're overwhelmed.

phrases.phrase: We're in this together
phrases.meaning: Reinforces a sense of shared responsibility.
phrases.example: We're in this together - let's figure it out as a team.

phrases.phrase: I appreciate you stepping up
phrases.example: I appreciate you stepping up when we were short-staffed.

phrases.phrase: Let's lean on each other's strengths
phrases.example: Let's lean on each other's strengths to finish this well.

phrases.phrase: Thanks for picking up the slack
phrases.example: Thanks for picking up the slack while I was out.

phrases.phrase: I owe you one for this
phrases.example: I owe you one for this, really appreciate it.

phrases.phrase: Let's tackle this as a team
phrases.meaning: Frames a challenge as a shared effort.
phrases.example: Let's tackle this as a team, not individually.

phrases.phrase: I'll back you up on this
phrases.meaning: Offers support in a discussion or decision.
phrases.example: I'll back you up on this in the meeting.

phrases.phrase: Two heads are better than one
phrases.example: Let's work on this together - two heads are better than one.

phrases.phrase: I'm just a message away if you need anything
phrases.example: I'm just a message away if you need anything today.

phrases.phrase: Let's make sure no one's left behind on this
phrases.meaning: Ensures inclusive progress across the team.
phrases.example: Let's make sure no one's left behind on this transition.

phrases.phrase: I appreciate you having my back
phrases.example: I appreciate you having my back during that call.

phrases.phrase: Let's check in on each other, not just the work
phrases.example: Let's check in on each other, not just the work, this week.

phrases.phrase: Teamwork makes the dream work
phrases.example: Great job everyone - teamwork makes the dream work!

phrases.phrase: Let's celebrate the small wins too
phrases.example: Let's celebrate the small wins too, not just the big ones.

phrases.phrase: I want everyone's voice to be heard
phrases.example: I want everyone's voice to be heard in this discussion.

phrases.phrase: Let's make decisions together as a group
phrases.example: Let's make decisions together as a group on this one.

phrases.phrase: We all bring something different to the table
phrases.example: We all bring something different to the table, and that's a strength.

phrases.phrase: Let's build on each other's ideas
phrases.example: Let's build on each other's ideas instead of starting from scratch.

phrases.phrase: I trust this team to figure it out
phrases.meaning: Expresses confidence in the group's ability.
phrases.example: I trust this team to figure it out together.

phrases.phrase: Let's keep each other accountable
phrases.example: Let's keep each other accountable on our commitments.

phrases.phrase: We're better together than apart
phrases.example: We're better together than apart on this kind of project.

phrases.phrase: Let's create space for everyone to contribute
phrases.example: Let's create space for everyone to contribute their ideas.

phrases.phrase: I love how this team supports each other
phrases.example: I love how this team supports each other during crunch time.

phrases.phrase: Let's make this a safe space to share ideas
phrases.example: Let's make this a safe space to share ideas, no bad ones here.

phrases.phrase: I want to hear from the quieter voices too
phrases.meaning: Invites input from less vocal team members.
phrases.example: I want to hear from the quieter voices too on this.

phrases.phrase: Let's give credit where it's due
phrases.example: Let's give credit where it's due - this was Priya's idea.

phrases.phrase: This team has great chemistry
phrases.meaning: Compliments how well a team works together.
phrases.example: This team has great chemistry, it shows in the results.

phrases.phrase: Let's keep building this trust with each other
phrases.example: Let's keep building this trust with each other over time.

phrases.phrase: I'm grateful to be part of this team
phrases.example: I'm grateful to be part of this team, honestly.

phrases.phrase: Let's keep the momentum going
phrases.example: Let's keep the momentum going into next quarter.

phrases.phrase: We win as a team and we learn as a team
phrases.meaning: Frames both success and failure as collective.
phrases.example: We win as a team and we learn as a team - no blame here.

phrases.phrase: Let's make sure we have fun along the way
phrases.example: Let's make sure we have fun along the way too.

phrases.phrase: Proud to work alongside all of you
phrases.example: Proud to work alongside all of you on this launch.

quiz.question: Which phrase suggests splitting tasks to work faster?
options: I owe you one for this
Let's celebrate the small wins too
quiz.explanation: This idiom means splitting work to be more efficient.

quiz.question: Which idiom encourages working together rather than alone?
options: Two heads are better than one
Let's sync up before we start
I'll take the lead on this part
Let's give credit where it's due
quiz.answer: Two heads are better than one
quiz.explanation: This idiom means collaboration produces better results than working solo.

quiz.question: Which phrase ensures fair recognition of contributions?
options: Let's give credit where it's due
Can you cover for me while I'm out?
I've got some bandwidth if you need support
Let's coordinate so we don't overlap
quiz.answer: Let's give credit where it's due
quiz.explanation: This ensures the right person is recognized for their work.

quiz.question: Thanks for picking up the ___ while I was out. (extra work)
quiz.explanation: 'Pick up the slack' means to take on extra work someone else couldn't do.

quiz.question: Which phrase promotes psychological safety in a team?
options: Let's make this a safe space to share ideas
I'll hand this off to you once I'm done
Can we pair up on this?
quiz.answer: Let's make this a safe space to share ideas
quiz.explanation: This encourages open, judgment-free sharing of ideas.
